title: Cuba says 4 people killed after gunfight with Florida speedboat
fullText: Deep snow impacts response to Massachusetts house explosion02:08Now PlayingUP NEXTCars sucked into sinkhole at Omaha intersection01:08FBI raids properties of Los Angeles schools chief01:27New questions over Epstein documents which have not been released by DOJ02:14Fourth grade class surprises student with uplifting messages01:16Trump focuses on economy in State of Union speech02:21Investigators return to Nancy Guthrie’s home01:29Teen who lost leg to cancer scores long-awaited basket01:30Trump set for State of the Union speech in primetime04:02New risks from historic blizzard01:55Savannah Guthrie announces $1 million reward for any information leading to her mother03:03Lawsuit alleges insurance company provider list is a “Ghost Network”03:31Texas Congressman faces new calls to resign after texts revealed01:34Record setting blizzard slams Northeast02:39Blizzard causes massive power outages in New Jersey00:55President Trump reacts after alarming security incident at Mar-a-Lago01:33More than 30 inches of snow in parts of Massachusetts00:56American tourists scramble amid cartel violence in Mexico01:45Historic blizzard grounds nearly all air travel in Northeast01:00Nightly NewsDeep snow impacts response to Massachusetts house explosion02:08Now PlayingCuba says 4 people killed after gunfight with Florida speedboat01:56UP NEXTCars sucked into sinkhole at Omaha intersection01:08FBI raids properties of Los Angeles schools chief01:27New questions over Epstein documents which have not been released by DOJ02:14Fourth grade class surprises student with uplifting messages01:16
summary: Cuba’s Interior Ministry said passengers on a speedboat registered in Florida opened fire on its border patrol agents, who returned fire and killed four people. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the U.S. will conduct an independent investigation. NBC News’ Jose Diaz-Balart reports.
